article.p: Learn how to set up a MetaOWL project and start building your first application.
Create a new MetaOWL project using the CLI:

pre.style: background: #1f2937; color: #f9fafb; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px;
pre.code: npm create metaowl@latest my-app cd my-app npm install

article.p: After creation, your project will have this structure:

pre.style: background: #1f2937; color: #f9fafb; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px;
pre.code: my-app/ ├── src/ │ ├── pages/ │ ├── components/ │ ├── layouts/ │ ├── metaowl.js │ └── index.html ├── package.json └── vite.config.js

pre.style: background: #1f2937; color: #f9fafb; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px;

article.p: Your app will be available at http://localhost:3000

pre.style: background: #1f2937; color: #f9fafb; padding: 16px; border-radius: 8px; overflow-x: auto; font-size: 14px; margin-bottom: 24px;

article.p: The output will be in the dist/ directory.
